summary: Added a new public header for the lbvector library module and changed lbvector.c to include it instead of carrying its dependency includes directly. The header collected Dolphin matrix/GX and HSD camera dependencies plus prototypes for lbvector functions and nearby unnamed functions, making the module easier to reference from other translation units during decompilation.
what_changed: Moved dependency includes for <dolphin/mtx/mtxtypes.h>, <dolphin/gx/GXTransform.h>, and "sysdolphin/baselib/cobj.h" out of lbvector.c and into the new header.
Changed lbvector.c to include "lbvector.h" at the top, while retaining <math.h> locally.
Declared lbvector vector math APIs such as lbvector_normalize, lbvector_normalize_xy, lbvector_add, lbvector_add_xy, lbvector_sub, lbvector_diff, lbvector_crossprod_normalized, and lbvector_rotate.

Declared several still-unnamed functions in the same module, including func_8000D620, func_8000D790, func_8000D8F4, func_8000DC6C, func_8000DCA8, func_8000DDAC, func_8000DE38, func_8000DF0C, func_8000DFF4, func_8000E138, func_8000E19C, func_8000E210, func_8000E530, and func_8000E838.
Added extern declarations for dependencies such as sqrtf_accurate, func_80022D1C, func_80022DBC, func_80022C30, and func_80369688.
smart_moves: Centralized lbvector declarations into a header so other source files can include typed prototypes instead of relying on ad hoc externs or duplicated declarations.
Kept module dependencies explicit in the header where exported prototypes require Vec, Mtx, MtxPtr, HSD_CObj, and Point3d types.
Reduced lbvector.c include clutter by replacing multiple type-provider includes with a single local module header.
Preserved unnamed function labels where no better names were evident, avoiding speculative naming.
decomp_lessons: When a C file's functions are needed across the project, extract a matching header that includes all type definitions required by its prototypes.
Header creation can be a low-risk organization step in decomp projects: it improves type sharing without necessarily changing function bodies or matching-sensitive codegen.
Do not over-name unknown functions just because they are being exposed in a header; keep func_-address names until behavior or source-derived names are known.
If exported prototypes use external SDK or engine types, the header must include the corresponding type headers rather than depending on transitive includes from users.
naming_conventions: Local library headers use the module name, here lbvector.h for lbvector.c.
Known vector helpers use the lbvector_ prefix, e.g. lbvector_len, lbvector_normalize, lbvector_add, lbvector_rotate.
Unknown functions retain address-based names in func_8000XXXX / func_80022XXX / func_80369688 form.
The new include guard uses lowercase module text with surrounding underscores: _lbvector_h_.
Pointer parameters are written in the existing style as Vec *a, HSD_CObj *cobj, and Point3d *screenCoords.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or codegen-matching tactic is directly evidenced beyond avoiding function body changes.
The PR appears focused on declaration and include organization, which should be safe for matching if prototypes match existing definitions.
Adding prototypes can help the compiler type-check cross-file calls and reduce implicit declaration issues without altering lbvector.c logic.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_8000D620 or other lbvector func_8000XXXX symbols.
Find later changes to lbvector.h include guard or prototype qualifiers.
Search for consumers that began including src/melee/lb/lbvector.h after this PR.
Check whether static prototypes like lbvector_len, lbvector_len_xy, sin, and cos remained in the header or were later moved back to the C file.
